LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF MAX SNYDER'S AMENDED CHARGE TO THE DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING RELATES BACK TO HIS ORIGINAL DFEH CHARGE. SNYDER'S ORIGINAL ALLEGATIONS THAT HE WAS TERMINATED FOR SEEKING MEDICAL LEAVE ARE CONSISTENT WITH THEORIES OF DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION AND FAILURE TO ACCOMMODATE; THE AMENDED CHARGE ALLEGED THE MEDICAL LEAVE WAS RELATED TO SNYDER'S DISABILITY. THIS IS DIFFERENT FROM RODRIGUEZ V. AIRBORNE EXPRESS (9TH CIR. 2001) 265 F.3D 890, 899, WHERE THE TWO FORMS OF ALLEGED DISCRIMINATION WERE RACE AND DISABILITY, NOT DISABILITY AND MEDICAL LEAVE. SNYDER'S ORIGINAL CHARGE ALLEGED HARASSMENT AND RETALIATION, SO SUCH ALLEGATIONS IN THE AMENDED CHARGE RELATE BACK AS WELL. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE (TRANSACTION ID # 100063732)

BASED ON THE COMPLAINT AND JUDICIALLY NOTICED DOCUMENTS, PLAINTIFF TO DATE HAS FAILED TO EXHAUST ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES AS TO HIS DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION ALLEGATIONS. (SEE WILLS V. SUP. CT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600, maria.torreanom@gmail.com (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Pacific Gas and Electric Company's demurrer to the second amended complaint is OVERRULED. Plaintiff Max Snyder's amended charge to the Department of Fair Employment and Housing relates back to his original DFEH charge. Snyder's original allegations that he was terminated for seeking medical leave are consistent with theories of disability discrimination and failure to accommodate; the amended charge alleged the medical leave was related to Snyder's disability. This is different from Rodriguez v. Airborne Express (9th Cir. 2001) 265 F.3d 890, 899, where the two forms of alleged discrimination were race and disability, not disability and medical leave. Snyder's original charge alleged harassment and retaliation, so such allegations in the amended charge relate back as well. Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tenative ruling.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Sep-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Stewart C. Altemus of Altemus & Wagner, Tel. No. 1-530-242-8800 appearing
for the Plaintiff by telephone appearance;
Atty. Aurelio Perez & Atty. Andrea R. Ortega, Tel. No. 1-415-288-6394 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Pacific Gas & Electric Company's demurrers to the first through fifth causes of action of the first amended complaint are SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Based on the complaint and judicially noticed documents, plaintiff to date has failed to exhaust administrative remedies as to his disability discrimination allegations. (See Wills v. Sup. Ct. (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 143, 155-57.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/ru
